sn1.71:1.2: As she was standing to one side, a devatā recited this verse to the Blessed One:
sn1.71:2.1: “Having killed what do you sleep in ease?
sn1.71:2.2: Having killed what do you not grieve?
sn1.71:2.3: Of the slaying of what one thing
sn1.71:3.1: The Buddha: “Having killed anger you sleep in ease.
sn1.71:3.2: Having killed anger you do not grieve.
sn1.71:3.3: The noble ones praise the slaying of anger
sn1.71:3.6: for having killed it you do not grieve.”
